get.description: Returns the current API exposable Fidesops configuration.

schema.title: Response Get Config Api V1 Config Get

get.description: Returns all connection configurations in the database.

patch.description:
Given a list of connection config data elements, create or update corresponding ConnectionConfig objects
or report failure

If the key in the payload exists, it will be used to update an existing ConnectionConfiguration.
Otherwise, a new ConnectionConfiguration will be created for you.

Note that ConnectionConfiguration.secrets are not updated through this endpoint.

get.description: Returns connection configuration with matching key.

delete.description: Removes the connection configuration with matching key.

put.description:
Update secrets that will be used to connect to a specified connection_type.

The specific secrets will be connection-dependent. For example, the components needed to connect to a Postgres DB
will differ from Dynamo DB.

get.description: Endpoint to test a connection at any time using the saved configuration secrets.

put.description:
Run validations against a dataset without attempting to save it to the database.

Checks that:
- all required fields are present, all field values are valid types
- all DataCategory values reference known keys
- etc.

After validating, also tests to see if the dataset is traversable. Note that
it's possible for a dataset to be valid but not traversable; this happens
when a dataset is dependent on references to other datasets.

Returns a 200 OK for all valid datasets, and a traversal_details object with
information about the traversal (or traversal errors).

get.description: Returns all datasets in the database.

patch.description:
Given a list of dataset elements, create or update corresponding Dataset objects
or report failure

Use for bulk creating and/or updating datasets.

If the fides_key for a given dataset exists, it will be treated as an update.
Otherwise, a new dataset will be created.

get.description: Returns a single dataset based on the given key.

delete.description: Removes the dataset based on the given key.

post.description:
Given a drp privacy request body, create and execute
a corresponding Fidesops PrivacyRequest

get.description:
Returns PrivacyRequest information where the respective privacy request is associated with
a policy that implements a Data Rights Protocol action.

schema.title: Response Get Encryption Key Api V1 Cryptography Encryption Key Get

put.description: Masks the value(s) provided using the provided masking strategy

get.description: Lists available masking strategies with instructions on how to use them

schema.title: Response List Masking Strategies Api V1 Masking Strategy Get

post.description:
Returns an access token if given credentials are correct, raises 401
exception if not
Creates a new client and returns the credentials

delete.description:
Deletes the client associated with the client_id. Does nothing if the client does
not exist

get.description: Returns a list of the scopes associated with the client. Returns an empty list if client does not exist.

schema.title: Response Get Client Scopes Api V1 Oauth Client  Client Id  Scope Get

put.description: Overwrites the client's scopes with those provided. Does nothing if the client doesn't exist

get.description: Returns a list of all scopes available for assignment in the system

schema.title: Response Read Scopes Api V1 Oauth Scope Get

get.description: Return a paginated list of all Policy records in this system

patch.description:
Given a list of policy data elements, create or update corresponding Policy objects
or report failure

get.description: Return a single Policy

patch.description:
Given a list of Rule data elements, create or update corresponding Rule objects
or report failure

Given a list of Rule data elements, create corresponding Rule objects
or report failure

get.description:
Return a paginated list of all Pre-Execution Webhooks that will run in order for the Policy **before** a
Privacy Request is executed.

put.summary: Create Or Update Pre Execution Webhooks
put.description:
Create or update the list of Policy Pre-Execution Webhooks that run **before** query execution.

All webhooks must be included in the request in the desired order. Any missing webhooks
from the request body will be removed.

schema.title: Response Create Or Update Pre Execution Webhooks Api V1 Policy  Policy Key  Webhook Pre Execution Put

get.description:
Return a paginated list of all Post-Execution Webhooks that will run in order for the Policy **after** a
Privacy Request is executed.

put.summary: Create Or Update Post Execution Webhooks
put.description:
Create or update the list of Policy Post-Execution Webhooks that run **after** query execution.

All webhooks must be included in the request in the desired order. Any missing webhooks
from the request body will be removed.

schema.title: Response Create Or Update Post Execution Webhooks Api V1 Policy  Policy Key  Webhook Post Execution Put

get.description: Loads the given Pre-Execution Webhook on the Policy

delete.description: Delete the Pre-Execution Webhook from the Policy and reorder remaining webhooks as necessary.

patch.description:
PATCH a single Policy Pre-Execution Webhook that runs **prior** to executing the Privacy Request.

Note that updates to the webhook's "order" can affect the order of the other pre-execution webhooks.

get.description: Loads the given Post-Execution Webhook on the Policy

delete.description: Delete the Post-Execution Webhook from the Policy and reorder remaining webhooks as necessary.

patch.description:
PATCH a single Policy Post-Execution Webhook that runs **after** executing the Privacy Request.

Note that updates to the webhook's "order" can affect the order of the other post-execution webhooks.

get.description:
Returns PrivacyRequest information. Supports a variety of optional query params.

To fetch a single privacy request, use the request_id query param `?request_id=`.
To see individual execution logs, use the verbose query param `?verbose=True`.

post.description:
Given a list of privacy request data elements, create corresponding PrivacyRequest objects
or report failure and execute them within the Fidesops system.

You cannot update privacy requests after they've been created.

get.description: Returns all the execution logs associated with a given privacy request ordered by updated asc.

put.description:
Returns dry run queries given a list of dataset ids.  If a dataset references another dataset, both dataset
keys must be in the request body.

schema.title: Response Get Request Preview Queries Api V1 Privacy Request Preview Put

post.description: Resume running a privacy request after it was paused by a Pre-Execution webhook

patch.description: Approve and dispatch a list of privacy requests and/or report failure
Deny a list of privacy requests and/or report failure

post.description:
Uploads data from an access request to specified storage destination.
Returns location of data.

get.description: Retrieves configs for storage.

patch.description:
Given a list of storage destination elements, create or update corresponding StorageConfig objects
or report failure.

put.description: Add or update secrets for storage config.

get.description: Retrieves configs for storage by key.

put.description:
Uses the SaaSConfig Pydantic model to validate the SaaS config
without attempting to save it to the database.

Checks that:
- all required fields are present, all field values are valid types
- each connector_param only has one of references or identity, not both

get.description: Returns the SaaS config for the given connection config.

delete.description:
Removes the SaaS config for the given connection config.
The corresponding dataset and secrets must be deleted before deleting the SaaS config

patch.description:
Given a SaaS config element, update the corresponding ConnectionConfig object
or report failure

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all users

post.description: Create a user given a username and password

get.description: Returns a User based on an Id

put.description:
Update a user given a `user_id`. By default this is limited to users
updating their own data.

delete.description: Deletes the User and associated ClientDetail if applicable

post.description:
Update a user's password given a `user_id`. By default this is limited to users
updating their own data.
Login the user by creating a client if it doesn't exist, and have that client generate a token
Logout the user by deleting its client

AccessLevel.title: AccessLevel
AccessLevel.description:
Perms given to the ConnectionConfig.  For example, with "read" permissions, fidesops promises
to not modify the data on a connected application database in any way.

"Write" perms mean we can update/delete items in the connected database.

AccessToken.description: A wrapper for the access_code returned upon successful authentication

A named variable which can be sourced from identities, dataset references, or connector params. These values

RuleCreate.title: RuleCreate
RuleCreate.description:
The schema to use when creating a Rule. This schema accepts a storage_destination_key
over a composite object.

RuleResponse.title: RuleResponse
RuleResponse.description:
The schema to use when returning a Rule via the API. This schema uses a censored version
of the `PolicyMaskingSpec` that omits the configuration to avoid exposing secrets.

RuleTarget.title: RuleTarget
RuleTarget.description: An external representation of a Rule's target DataCategory within a Fidesops Policy

SaaSConfig.title: SaaSConfig
SaaSConfig.description:
Used to store endpoint and param configurations for a SaaS connector.
This is done to separate the details of how to make the API calls
from the data provided by a given API collection.

The required fields for the config are converted into a Dataset which is
merged with the standard Fidesops Dataset to provide a complete set of dependencies
for the graph traversal.

SaaSConfigValidationDetails.title: SaaSConfigValidationDetails
SaaSConfigValidationDetails.description: Message with any validation issues with the SaaS config

SaaSRequest.title: SaaSRequest
SaaSRequest.description:
A single request with static or dynamic path, headers, query, and body params.
Also specifies the names and sources for the param values needed to build the request.

Includes optional strategies for postprocessing and pagination.

SaaSSchema.title: SaaSSchema
SaaSSchema.description:
Abstract base schema for updating SaaS connection configuration secrets.
Fields are added during runtime based on the connector_params in the
passed in saas_config

SnowflakeDocsSchema.title: SnowflakeDocsSchema
SnowflakeDocsSchema.description: Snowflake Secrets Schema for API Docs

StorageSecretsS3Docs.title: StorageSecretsS3Docs
StorageSecretsS3Docs.description: The secrets required to connect to S3, for documentation

Strategy.description: General shape for swappable strategies (ex: auth, processors, pagination, etc.)

UserCreate.description: Data required to create a FidesopsUser

UserLogin.description: Similar to UserCreate except we do not need the extra validation on username and password

UserLoginResponse.description: Similar to UserResponse except with an access token

UserPasswordReset.description: Contains both old and new passwords when resetting a password

UserUpdate.description: Data required to update a FidesopsUser

ValidateDatasetResponse.title: ValidateDatasetResponse
ValidateDatasetResponse.description:
Response model for validating a dataset, which includes both the dataset
itself (if valid) plus a details object describing if the dataset is
traversable or not.

ValidateSaaSConfigResponse.description:
Response model for validating a SaaS config, which includes both the SaaS config
itself (if valid) plus a details object describing any validation errors.

WebhookOrder.description: Schema for displaying a minimal amount of information about the webhook and its order
